key_quote: C++ atomics achieves only 9% exact-match (1/11) while aggregate 81.2% hides per-category failure — a developer using std::atomic experiences 9% accuracy
rationale: All originally-identified issues resolved plus B2 math rigor pass complete. C++ atomics fixed (9% → 90%), benchmark expanded to 203 snippets (96.6% exact, 98.0% top-3), differential tests honestly separated (498 meaningful + 2,280 trivial), fence costs reframed as analytical weights, Theorem 2 explicitly acknowledged as trivially true with NP-hardness remark for whole-program version, Theorem 3 corrected, GPU has 108/108 SMT validation, build/CI integration added, unrecognized pattern warnings with coverage_confidence metric, Dartagnan comparison table added, all stale numbers fixed (Discussion/Conclusion had 89.7%/94.6%/13-of-21/96.1% from older versions). Remaining limitations: theorems are paper proofs (not mechanized), pattern-level safety does not compose to program-level.
resolved_issues: Differential tests: honestly separated (498 meaningful + 2,280 trivial)
Fence costs: reframed as analytical weights
Theorem 2: explicitly acknowledged as trivially true; NP-hardness remark added
Build integration: pip install + CLI + GitHub Action
B2: Fixed all stale numbers in Discussion/Conclusion (89.7→96.6, 94.6→98.0, 13/21→7/7, 96.1→98.0)
B2: Added unrecognized pattern warning with coverage_confidence metric
B2: Added CI/CD integration section in paper
B2: Added Dartagnan comparison table in paper
B2: Honest GPU model counting (5 independent, not 10)
B2: Fixed appendix false-negative table (was 18, now 7 matching main body)
B2: Removed directory structure references from paper body
B2: Complete math rigor pass on all 3 theorems
